name: Awadhi
notes: Awadhi (अवधी), an Eastern Hindi / Indo-Aryan language of the Awadh region of Uttar Pradesh, India (the language of Tulsidas's Ramcharitmanas). STUB: the grapheme inventory is NOT yet specified. Script: Devanagari, an abugida — consonant letters carry an inherent vowel (schwa) that is cancelled by virama ⟨्⟩ and overridden by dependent vowel signs (matras); a full spec needs the inherent-vowel/schwa-deletion rules, conjunct (ligature) handling, nukta letters and anusvara/candrabindu nasalisation, which differ from those of standard Hindi. Awadhi is also historically written in the Kaithi and Perso-Arabic scripts; input is assumed to be Devanagari. PHONEME INVENTORY: attested inventory from PHOIBLE (glottocode awad1243), declared directly. `graphemes` stays empty because none of that depends on the writing system: the sounds of this language are known regardless of whether — or how — it is written.
